# UC-22.2.44 · NIS2 Cooperation Group and Sector Information Sharing Participation

## Description

Correlates the cooperation-forum register with closed ServiceNow meeting tasks and STIX indicator volume per named feed so lapses in sector ISAC participation or silent intel pipelines become visible.

## Value

Public affairs and cyber leaders can demonstrate active collaboration and functioning information exchanges without maintaining separate spreadsheets, which matters when policymakers ask for proof of collective resilience investments.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain `nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv` with `group_id` aligned to ServiceNow `assignment_group` and intel `feed_name`. (2) Ingest STIX via ES TAXII or HEC. (3) Tune `feed_floor` per forum. (4) Monthly CISO review. (5) Store exports in `index=audit_evidence`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise Security ([Splunkbase 263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)) installed on search heads handling `index*=*threat*` lookups — confirm 12-month license for intel features.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) pulling task/incident tables with `assignment_group`, `short_description`, `closed_at` within 300-second poll default.
• HEC token on heavy forwarder if a national CSIRT pushes STIX JSON directly; set `indexes = threat_intel` in `inputs.conf`.
• Role includes `threat_intel`, `itsm` indexes; ES analysts need `ess_user`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map each ISAC to both a ServiceNow group and intel `feed_name`. For ES TAXII, use Intelligence Downloads UI (`Enterprise Security` > `Intelligence` > `Downloads`) pointing at `/api/v1/taxii2/` endpoints — retain `sourcetype=stix`. For ServiceNow, validate REST path `/api/now/table/task` returns your cooperation templates. Document expected indicator volume (e.g., ≥1000 unique `indicator_id` per 30 days for high-trust feeds). Store `nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv` under change control.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run daily with `earliest=-30d` on intel subsearch.

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv
| join group_id type=left [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task (short_description="*ISAC*" OR short_description="*cooperation group*") earliest=-180d
    | stats latest(state) as last_meeting_state latest(closed_at) as last_meeting_date by assignment_group
    | rename assignment_group as group_id
]
| eval participation_gap=if(last_meeting_state!="Closed" OR isnull(last_meeting_date),1,0)
| join group_id type=left [
    search index=threat_intel sourcetype=stix earliest=-30d
    | stats dc(indicator_id) as indicators_consumed by feed_name
    | rename feed_name as group_id
]
```

Threshold `indicators_consumed` against per-forum floors from lookup. Alert when `participation_gap=1` OR intel floor breached 3 consecutive days.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick one forum: verify latest meeting closure timestamp against ServiceNow UI. Compare `dc(indicator_id)` to ES Intelligence view for same `feed_name`. If divergent, check `_indextime - _time` lag >24h — often parser delay.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard with map of forums to owners, Slack notification including `group_id`, monthly PDF archived to `index=audit_evidence`. Runbook cites NDA constraints when attaching raw indicators.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Null intel join** — wrong index name; some deployments use `threat_intel` vs `threat_activity` — align macros.
• **ServiceNow API 429** — lower modular input interval or add retry in add-on.
• **Indicator flood skews dc** — dedup noise via `where threat_actor!="unknown"` after validating with threat lead.
• **RBAC denied** — curator role missing `threat_intel`; update `authorize.conf`.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv
| lookup nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds group_id OUTPUT feed_floor_threshold exception_until
| eval feed_floor_threshold=coalesce(feed_floor_threshold,1000)
| join group_id type=left [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task (short_description="*ISAC*" OR short_description="*cooperation group*") earliest=-180d
    | stats latest(state) as last_meeting_state latest(closed_at) as last_meeting_date by assignment_group
    | rename assignment_group as group_id
]
| eval participation_gap=if(last_meeting_state!="Closed" OR isnull(last_meeting_date),1,0)
| join group_id type=left [
    search index=threat_intel sourcetype=stix earliest=-30d
    | stats dc(indicator_id) as indicators_consumed by feed_name
    | rename feed_name as group_id
]
| eval indicators_consumed=coalesce(indicators_consumed,0)
| where participation_gap=1 OR indicators_consumed < feed_floor_threshold
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table group_id, last_meeting_state, last_meeting_date, indicators_consumed, participation_gap, feed_floor_threshold
```

## Visualization

Participation gap table, timeline of meetings, single value active STIX feeds.
